summary: First testing run of RoopCraft OS MVP. Backend: 23/23 pytest tests PASSED (100%) — all auth (register/login/me/dup-email/bad-password), auth-guard (401), dashboard stats, lead CRUD, LLM-driven report generation (model=gemini), sales-kit generation, and proposal generation return the expected JSON shapes with all required keys. LLM endpoints returned a transient 'Budget exceeded' error on the very first run, but succeeded on the immediate re-run and generated well-formed reports (business_summary, google_maps_analysis, instagram_audit, nearby_opportunities, competitor_analysis, growth_strategy, reel_ideas). Frontend: sign-up → auto-signin → dashboard flow works; bottom tabs (Dashboard/Leads/Reports/Profile/New) all navigate; Profile shows correct name/email; New-Audit form has all testIDs (name/city/state/generate/model-gemini/claude/openai) and category picker modal opens with option testIDs. Deprecated packages detected: none. React Native web deprecation warnings observed for legacy 'shadow*' style props and 'props.pointerEvents' — main agent should migrate to boxShadow / style.pointerEvents. TestID naming mismatch on dashboard stat cards vs review-request spec (see action items). Sign-out click did not visibly navigate to /sign-in in test run — needs main-agent verification.

minor.issue: Returns 403 Forbidden instead of 401 Unauthorized when Authorization header is missing. FastAPI HTTPBearer default. Test accepts 401/403, but strict spec says 401.

ui_bugs.issue: testID naming inconsistent with review spec. Actual: stat-meetings, stat-proposals, stat-active, stat-win-rate. Expected (per spec): stat-meetings-scheduled, stat-proposals-sent, stat-active-clients. Rename for consistency.

Tab bar items rely on default expo-router Tabs.Screen buttons; no testID on individual tab buttons. Playwright had to fall back to text selectors (Dashboard/Leads/Reports/Profile). Consider using tabBarButtonTestID or a custom tabBar for reliable e2e selectors.

After tapping profile-signout-button on web, the URL remained at /new-audit and the sign-in form did not appear on the following screen. This may be a test-context artifact (previous form state) but should be verified by the main agent — expected behavior is redirect to /(auth)/sign-in after clearing token.

issues: 'shadow*' style props deprecated warning — migrate to boxShadow for RN web compatibility.
'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' — likely from a Modal or overlay component.

action_items: Rename dashboard stat card testIDs to match spec: stat-meetings-scheduled, stat-proposals-sent, stat-active-clients (currently stat-meetings, stat-proposals, stat-active) in /app/frontend/app/(app)/dashboard.tsx.
Verify profile sign-out redirects to /(auth)/sign-in on web. If router.replace to sign-in is not called after signOut(), add it.

Migrate deprecated RN-web style props: replace shadow* props with boxShadow, and props.pointerEvents with style.pointerEvents (likely in Modal/overlay components under src/ or app/(app)/new-audit.tsx).
Optionally: change HTTPBearer(auto_error=True) behavior so /api/leads returns 401 instead of 403 when Authorization header is missing (spec says 401).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: `logger` is defined at line 509 but used inside route handlers (e.g., logger.exception in create_lead at line 383) which are defined earlier. Because logger.exception is only called on exception paths at request-time (not import-time), this works, but it's fragile. Move logging.basicConfig + logger definition to the top of the module.
server.py: DELETE /api/leads/{id} returns 200 with {success:true}; typically DELETE returns 204 No Content. Minor.
server.py: extract_json falls back to {'raw': text} on parse failure — good defensive handling; tests already assert 'raw' NOT in report.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (all core flows work; sign-out redirect + stat-card testID names need verification/fix)

seed_data_creation: Created unique TEST_-prefixed users via /api/auth/register per test run. In lead_crud_seeded_test.py, a lead is inserted directly into MongoDB (bypassing LLM) with an id prefixed with a UUID and business_name 'TEST_Seeded Cafe' — it is DELETEd at the end of the module.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend + LLM integrations are fully green. If retesting, run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --tb=short`. Emergent LLM key showed a transient 'Budget exceeded' response on first request but succeeded on retry — if seen again, simply rerun. Focus next iteration on: (a) verifying sign-out UX redirect on web, (b) confirming the renamed dashboard stat-card testIDs, and (c) end-to-end frontend audit-generation once main agent confirms LLM budget is stable.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no critical bugs. The initial 'AI report generation failed: Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0, Max budget: 0.0' was transient (litellm/OpenAI wrapper) and self-recovered on immediate retry (all 3 LLM endpoints then succeeded). If it recurs persistently, the Emergent LLM key/budget will need main-agent-side investigation, not code changes.
